Learning Outcome: Know that the Disaster Recovery Plan exists and is managed by IT; recovery strategy relies on SaaS provider redundancy and automated failover; Google Workspace (SSO/identity) is the top recovery priority; if a service is unavailable or you suspect an outage, raise a flag in Slack #general, DM an IT team member or your manager, or email security@civicactions.com

Use the communication hierarchy during a DR event: Primary (Slack chat, Zoom video), Secondary (Google Chat), Tertiary (Slack video, Zoom chat); understand that clients/partners are notified only if deliverables are impacted, coordinated by leadership

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Learning Outcome: Know the primary and fallback communication channels for each critical system (Google Workspace, Slack, Zoom, Kandji/Iru, Atlassian, Unanet, GitHub, GitLab, Rippling, Culture Amp); identify the organization actions for each during an outage

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Learning Outcome: Understand that primary backups are managed by SaaS providers; ensure administrative access is limited and documented; use export/backup features where available; do not depend on a single platform for critical documentation

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Learning Outcome: Understand that the DRP is reviewed annually, vendor DR assurances are reviewed annually, and tabletop exercises are conducted as appropriate

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Learning Outcome: Document timeline and impact after a significant incident; review response effectiveness; identify improvement actions; track through the continual improvement process
